manual_entries.title: Cooling Zone State - Primary Airflow Setpoint Mapping
manual_entries.summary: In cooling mode, the cooling loop output is mapped to the active primary airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active primary airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint and the series fan airflow setpoint shall be no higher than OA-min.

structured_payload.title: Cooling Zone State - Primary Airflow Setpoint Mapping
structured_payload.section_title: When the Zone State Is Cooling
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, variable-volume fan
structured_payload.summary: In cooling mode, the cooling loop output is mapped to the active primary airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active primary airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint and the series fan airflow setpoint shall be no higher than OA-min.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Map cooling loop output to active primary airflow setpoint from minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint. If AHU SAT > room temperature, set primary airflow setpoint ≤ minimum endpoint and series fan airflow setpoint ≤ OA-min.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: a. The cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active primary airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. 1. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active primary airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint and the series fan airflow setpoint shall be no higher than OA-min.

structured_payload.title: VAV Damper Modulation
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, variable-volume fan
structured_payload.summary: The VAV damper is modulated to maintain the measured airflow at setpoint.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Always active when airflow setpoint is non-zero
structured_payload.required_behavior: Modulate VAV damper to maintain measured airflow at setpoint.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: 5.10.5.4. The VAV damper shall be modulated to maintain the measured airflow at setpoint.

chunk.cleaned_text:
(Directly
controlling heating off zone temperature control loop is not acceptable).
5.10.5.4.  The VAV damper shall be modulated to maintain the measured airflow at setpoint .
5.10.5.5.  Fan Control . Fan shall run whenever zone is in heating  or cooling Zone State , or if the associated
Zone Group  is in Occupied Mode . Prior to starting the fan, the damper is first driven fully closed to
ensure that the fan is not rotating backward.  Once the fan is proven ON for a fixed time delay (15
seconds), t he damper override is released.
5.10.6.  Alarms
5.10.6.1.  Low Primary Airflow
a. If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater
than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.
b. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater
than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm.

manual_entries.title: Fan Control - Run Conditions and Start Sequence
manual_entries.summary: Fan runs whenever zone is in heating or cooling Zone State, or if the associated Zone Group is in Occupied Mode. Prior to starting the fan, the damper is driven fully closed to prevent backward rotation. After a fixed time delay (15 seconds) with fan proven ON, the damper override is released.

Prior to starting the fan, the damper is driven fully closed to prevent backward rotation. After a fixed time delay (15 seconds) with fan proven ON, the damper override is released.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Zone State is Heating or Cooling, or Zone Group is in Occupied Mode

when the primary air volume is less than Zone -Abs-OA-min for 1 minute, and shall shut off
when primary air volume is above Zone -Abs-OA-min by 10% for 3 minutes.
The designer must ensure that the sum of the indirect ventilation provided by the fan plus the ventilation provided by
the primary air at minimum setpoint  meet Standard 62.1 requirements.

5.5.8.1.  Cooling SAT Reset Requests

after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20, send 3 requests.

and after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Secti on 5.1.20 , send 2 requests.

5.13.8.1.  Cooling SAT Reset Requests
a. If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint  by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and
after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20 , send 3 requests.
b. Else if the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint  by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes
and after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20 , send 2 requests.
c. Else if the Cooling Loop  is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the Cooling Loop  is less than
85%.
d. Else if the Cooling Loop  is less than  95%, send 0 requests.
5.13.8.2.  Cold -Duct Static Pressure Reset Requests
a. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint  while setpoint  is greater than zero and the
damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 3 requests.
b.

chunk.cleaned_text:
ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  105 5.13.8.  System Requests
5.13.8.1.  Cooling SAT Reset Requests
a. If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint  by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and
after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20 , send 3 requests.
b. Else if the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint  by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes
and after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20 , send 2 requests.
c. Else if the Cooling Loop  is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the Cooling Loop  is less than
85%.
d. Else if the Cooling Loop  is less than  95%, send 0 requests.
5.13.8.2.  Cold -Duct Static Pressure Reset Requests
a. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint  while setpoint  is greater than zero and the
damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 3 requests.
b.

manual_entries.title: Cooling SAT Reset Requests from Dual-Duct VAV Zone
manual_entries.summary: If zone temperature exceeds cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes, send 3 requests. Else if exceeds by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes, send 2 requests. Else if Cooling Loop > 95%, send 1 request until loop < 85%. Else send 0 requests.

structured_payload.title: Cooling SAT Reset Requests from Dual-Duct VAV Zone

manual_entries.title: Temperature Control - Cooling Mode
manual_entries.summary: In cooling mode, the cooling loop output resets the cold duct airflow setpoint from minimum to cooling maximum; the cold duct damper modulates to maintain measured airflow at setpoint.

structured_payload.title: Temperature Control - Cooling Mode
structured_payload.summary: In cooling mode, the cooling loop output resets the cold duct airflow setpoint from minimum to cooling maximum; the cold duct damper modulates to maintain measured airflow at setpoint.
structured_payload.required_behavior: The Cooling Loop output shall reset the active cold duct airflow setpoint from minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. The cold duct damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured cold duct airflow at the active cold duct airflow setpoint.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: When the Zone State is cooling, the Cooling Loop output shall reset the active cold duct airflow setpoint from minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. The cold duct damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured cold duct airflow at the active cold duct airflow setpoint.

is indirect via return air from interior zones  that are overventilated due to the outdoor air economizer.

manual_entries.title: Temperature Control - Heating Mode
manual_entries.summary: In heating mode, the heating loop output resets the hot duct airflow setpoint from zero to heating maximum; the hot duct damper modulates to maintain that airflow; the cooling damper maintains total airflow at minimum.

structured_payload.title: Temperature Control - Heating Mode
structured_payload.summary: In heating mode, the heating loop output resets the hot duct airflow setpoint from zero to heating maximum; the hot duct damper modulates to maintain that airflow; the cooling damper maintains total airflow at minimum.
structured_payload.required_behavior: The heating-loop output shall reset the active hot duct airflow setpoint from zero to the heating maximum endpoint. The hot duct damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured hot duct airflow at the active hot duct airflow setpoint. The cooling damper shall be controlled to maintain the sum of the measured inlet airflows at the minimum endpoint.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: When the Zone State is heating, the heating-loop output shall reset the active hot duct airflow setpoint from zero to the heating maximum endpoint. The hot duct damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured hot duct airflow at the active hot duct airflow setpoint. The cooling damper shall be controlled to maintain the sum of the measured inlet airflows at the minimum endpoint.

ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  97 5.11.6.2.  Airflow Sensor Calibration . If the fan serving the zone is off and airflow sensor readin g is above
the larger of 10% of the maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 30 minutes , generate a Level 3
alarm.
5.11.6.3.  Leaking Damper.  If the damper position is 0 %, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of
10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the
damper is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm.
The constant value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the
transducer and A/D converter used to measure air flow.  The value used should be determined as the minimum
accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.
5.11.7.  Testing/Commissioning Overrides . Provide software switches that interlock to a system level point to
a. force  zone airflow setpoint  to zero ,
b. force  zone airflow setpoint  to Vcool -max,
c.

chunk.cleaned_text:
ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  73 5.5.8.  System Requests
5.5.8.1.  Cooling SAT Reset Requests
a. If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint  by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and
after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20, send 3 requests.
b. Else if the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint  by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes
and after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Secti on 5.1.20 , send 2 requests.
c. Else if the Cooling Loop  is greater than 95%, send 1 request  until the Cooling Loop  is less than
85%.
d. Else if the Cooling Loop  is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
5.5.8.2.  Static Pressure Reset Requests
a. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint  while setpoint  is greater than zero and the
damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 3 requests.

because the discharge air will stratify rather than mix.

Dual -Duct VAV Terminal Unit  − Cold -Duct Minimum Control
Cold -duct minimum control logic is the most conventional, but least efficient, dual -duct control strategy. It ensures
ventilation rates without Standard 62.1 -2016 generalized multiple spaces considerations, because only the cold duct
has ventilation air with DFDD systems.
This strategy uses dual airflow sensors, one at each inlet. It may be used with or without DCV..

Air-Handling Unit System Modes
5.15.1.  AHU system modes  are the same as the mode  of the Zone Group  served by the system. When Zone Group
served by an air -handling system are in diffe rent modes, the following hierarchy applies (highest one sets
AHU mode ):
a. Occupied Mode
b. Cooldown Mode
c. Setup Mode
d. Warmup Mode
e. Setback Mode
f. Unoccupied Mode
Multiple -Zone VAV Air -Handling Unit

Dual -Duct VAV Terminal Unit  − Mixing Control with Inlet Airflow Sensors
Mixing control  logic is the preferred option for use with DCV.  If the box serves more than one room, it requires a
DD box with mixing capability ; a pair of single -duct boxes strapped together wi th a common plenum will not work
because the discharge air will stratify rather than mix.

5.15.1.  AHU system modes  are the same as the mode  of the Zone Group  served by the system. When Zone Group
served by an air -handling system are in diffe rent modes, the following hierarchy applies (highest one sets
AHU mode ):
a. Occupied Mode
b. Cooldown Mode
c. Setup Mode
d. Warmup Mode
e. Setback Mode
f. Unoccupied Mode

5.7.8.3.  If There Is  a Hot -Water Coil, Hot -Water Reset Requests
a. If the DAT  is 17°C (30°F) less than setpoint  for 5 minutes, send 3 requests.
b. Else if the DAT  is 8.3°C (15°F) less than setpoint  for 5 minutes, send 2 requests.
c. Else if HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request  until the HW valve position is
less than 85 %.
d. Else if the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

Dual -Duct VAV Terminal Unit  − Cold -Duct Minimum Control
Cold -duct minimum control logic is the most conventional, but least efficient, dual -duct control strategy. It ensures
ventilation rates without Standard 62.1 -2016 generalized multiple spaces considerations, because only the cold duct
has ventilation air with DFDD systems.
This strategy uses dual airflow sensors, one at each inlet. It may be used with or without DCV..

manual_entries.title: Dual-Duct VAV Temperature Control - Deadband Mode
manual_entries.summary: When Zone State is deadband, the active cold duct airflow setpoint is the minimum endpoint. The cold duct damper is modulated to maintain measured cold duct airflow at that setpoint. The hot duct damper is closed.

structured_payload.title: Dual-Duct VAV Temperature Control - Deadband Mode

endpoint . The cold duct damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured
cold duct airflow at the active cold duct airflow setpoint . The hot duct damper shall be closed.
The deadband air flow is maintained by the cooling damper, as the cooling system has a definite source of
ventilation. With dual -fan dual -duct, the heating fan generally has no direct ventilation source; typically, ventilation
is indirect via return air from interior zones  that are overventilated due to the outdoor air economizer.
c. When the Zone State  is heating, the Heating Loop  output shall be mapped to the hot duct
damper position.

manual_entries.title: Dual-Duct VAV Maximum Hot Duct Airflow Limiting
manual_entries.summary: Maximum hot duct airflow is limited by a reverse-acting P-only loop whose setpoint is the heating maximum endpoint and whose output is the maximum hot duct damper position ranging from 0% to 100%.

structured_payload.title: Dual-Duct VAV Maximum Hot Duct Airflow Limiting
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Dual-duct VAV terminal unit with discharge airflow sensor
structured_payload.summary: Maximum hot duct airflow is limited by a reverse-acting P-only loop whose setpoint is the heating maximum endpoint and whose output is the maximum hot duct damper position ranging from 0% to 100%.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Limit maximum hot duct airflow using a reverse-acting P-only loop with setpoint = Vheat-max. Output is maximum hot duct damper position (0-100%).
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Maximum hot duct airflow shall be limited by a reverse-acting P-only loop whose setpoint is the heating maximum endpoint and whose output is the maximum hot duct damper position ranging from 0% to 100%.

chunk.cleaned_text:
104 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  2. Maximum hot duct airflow shall be limited  by a reverse -acting P -only loop whose setpoint
is the heating maximum endpoint  and whose output is the maximum hot duct damper
position ranging from 0% to 100%.
Because the heating damper is operating in a pressure -dependent manner, a loop must be added to limit heating
damper position to Vheat -max. When this comes into pla y, the only air passing through the discharge airflow sensor
is heating air.
5.13.5.2.  Overriding Section 5.13.5.1  Logic  (to Avoid Backflow  from One Duct  to the Other )
a. If heat ing air handler is not proven ON, the heating damper shall be closed.
b. If cooling air handler is not proven ON, the cooling damper shall be closed.

The deadband air flow is maintained by the cooling damper, as the cooling system has a definite source of
ventilation. With dual -fan dual -duct, the heating fan generally has no direct ventilation source; typically, ventilation
is indirect via return air from interior zones  that are overventilated due to the outdoor air economizer.

ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  73 5.5.8.  System Requests
5.5.8.1.  Cooling SAT Reset Requests
a. If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint  by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and
after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20, send 3 requests.
b. Else if the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint  by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes
and after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Secti on 5.1.20 , send 2 requests.
c. Else if the Cooling Loop  is greater than 95%, send 1 request  until the Cooling Loop  is less than
85%.
d. Else if the Cooling Loop  is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
5.5.8.2.  Static Pressure Reset Requests
a. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint  while setpoint  is greater than zero and the
damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 3 requests.

Dual -Duct VAV Terminal Unit  − Mixing Control with Inlet Airflow Sensors
Mixing control  logic is the preferred option for use with DCV.  If the box serves more than one room, it requires a
DD box with mixing capability ; a pair of single -duct boxes strapped together wi th a common plenum will not work
because the discharge air will stratify rather than mix.

Dual -Duct VAV Terminal Unit  − Cold -Duct Minimum Control
Cold -duct minimum control logic is the most conventional, but least efficient, dual -duct control strategy. It ensures
ventilation rates without Standard 62.1 -2016 generalized multiple spaces considerations, because only the cold duct
has ventilation air with DFDD systems.
This strategy uses dual airflow sensors, one at each inlet. It may be used with or without DCV..

after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20 , send 3 requests.

and after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20 , send 2 requests.

85%.
d. Else if the Heating Loop  is less than 9 5%, send 0 requests.

response time of the building pressurization loop should not exceed 1/5 the closed -loop response time of the return -
fan control loop.

track airflow.)

manual_entries.title: Freeze Protection Stage 1
manual_entries.summary: If SAT drops below 4.4°C for 5 minutes, send two HHW plant requests, override OA damper to minimum, and modulate heating coil to maintain SAT at least 6°C. Disable when SAT rises above 7°C for 5 minutes.

structured_payload.title: Freeze Protection Stage 1
structured_payload.summary: If SAT drops below 4.4°C for 5 minutes, send two HHW plant requests, override OA damper to minimum, and modulate heating coil to maintain SAT at least 6°C. Disable when SAT rises above 7°C for 5 minutes.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Supply air temperature drops below 4.4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes.
structured_payload.required_behavior: If the supply air temperature drops below 4.4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, override the outdoor air damper to the minimum position, and modulate the heating coil to maintain a supply air temperature of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable this function when supply air temperature rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: If the supply air temperature drops below 4.4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, override the outdoor air damper to the minimum position, and modulate the heating coil to maintain a supply air temperature of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable this function when supply air temperature rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.

air damper to the minimum posit ion, and modulate the heating coil to maintain a supply air
temperature of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable this function when supply air temperature rises above
7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.
The first stage of freeze protection locks out the economizer. Most like ly this has already occurred by this time, but
this logic provides insurance.
5.16.12.2.  If the supply air temperature drops below 3.3°C (38°F) for 5 minutes, fully close both the
economizer damper and the minimum outdoor air damper for 1 hour and set a Level 3 alarm noting
that minimum ventilation was interrupted.
chunk.text_excerpt:
If the supply air temperature drops below 4.4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two (or more, as
required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot -water plant requests , override the outdoor
ai

evidence.evidence_text: If the supply air temperature drops below 4.4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, override the outdoor air damper to the minimum position, and modulate the heating coil to maintain a supply air temperature of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable this function when supply air temperature rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.

132 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  Heating coil is controlled to an air temperature setpoint . The sensors will not read accurately  with the fan OFF, but
they will be influenced by proximity to the heating coil. A temperature of 27°C (80°F) at either of these sensors
indicates that the interior of the unit is sufficiently warm. This avoids the situation where a fixed valve position le ads
to very high (and potentially damaging) temperatures inside the unit.

5.16.13.5.  Low bu ilding pressure (less than 0 Pa [0.0 in. of water], i.e., negative)  for 5 minutes : Level 4.
Automatic fault detection  and diagnostics  (AFDD) is a sophisticated system for detecting and diagnosing air -
handler faults.
To function correctly, AFDD  requires spe cific sensors and data be available, as detailed in the sequences below. If
this information is not available, AFDD tests that do not apply should be deleted.
5.16.14.  Automatic Fault Detection and Diagnostics
The AFDD routines for AHUs continually assess AHU performance by comparing the values of BAS inputs and
outputs to a subset of potential fault conditions. The subset of potential fault conditions that is assessed at any point
depends on the operating state (OS) of the AHU, as determined by the position of  the cooling and heating valves and
the economizer damper.

Minimum outdoor air control is enabled when economizer damper position is less than MOA -P because it cannot be
assumed that the combination of the minimum and the economizer outdoor air dampers are providing sufficient
outdoor air under these conditions.
Minimum outdoor air control is disabled when return damper position is less than MRA -P, because the economizer
damper has been closed to enable an accurate airflow measurement through the minimum outdoor air damper.
The 20% and 80% thresholds can be increased/decreased to ensure minimum outdoor airflow will be maintained
but at the expense of fan energy .

one system, while both AHUs are operating.
This prevents relief fans from fighting each other, which can lead to flow reversal or unstable fan speed control and
space pressurization problems.

determined by the engineer based on building geometry.

they will be influenced by proximity to the heating coil. A temperature of 27°C (80°F) at either of these sensors
indicates that the interior of the unit is sufficiently warm. This avoids the situation where a fixed valve position leads
to very high (and potentially damaging) temperatures inside the unit.

5.17.4.6.  For each air handler, the operator shall be able to suppress the alarm for any fault condition.
5.17.4.7.  Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended under the following conditions:
a. When AHU is not operating
b. For a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode (e.g., from Warmup Mode to
Occupied Mode ) of any Zone Group  served by the AHU
5.17.4.8.  A fault condition that evaluates as TRUE  must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before it
is reported to the operator.
5.17.4.9.  Test mode shall temporarily set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for a period of
TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing of the AFDD system and ensure normal fault
detection occurs after testing is complete.
5.17.4.10.  When a fault condition is reported to the operator, it shall be a Level 3 alarm and shall include the
description of the fault and the list of possible diagnoses from Table 5.17.4.5 .

manual_entries.title: AFDD: Temperature Rise Across Inactive Heating Coil (FC#3)
manual_entries.summary: Fault condition when return air minus supply air temperature exceeds combined error threshold plus fan heat, and heating coil valve is at 0%.

structured_payload.title: AFDD: Temperature Rise Across Inactive Heating Coil (FC#3)
structured_payload.summary: Fault condition when return air minus supply air temperature exceeds combined error threshold plus fan heat, and heating coil valve is at 0%.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: RAT_avg - SAT_avg ≥ sqrt(εSAT² + εRAT²) + ΔTSF AND HC = 0%
structured_payload.required_behavior: Report fault condition with possible diagnoses: HCET sensor error, HCLT sensor error, heating coil valve stuck open or leaking, gas/electric heat stuck on.

manual_entries.title: AFDD: Evaluation Suspension Conditions
manual_entries.summary: Evaluation of fault conditions is suspended when AHU is not operating and for ModeDelay minutes after a change in mode of any Zone Group served.

structured_payload.title: AFDD: Evaluation Suspension Conditions
structured_payload.summary: Evaluation of fault conditions is suspended when AHU is not operating and for ModeDelay minutes after a change in mode of any Zone Group served.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: AHU not operating OR mode change of any Zone Group
structured_payload.required_behavior: Suspend AFDD evaluation for ModeDelay minutes after mode change.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended under the following conditions: a. When AHU is not operating b. For a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode (e.g., from Warmup Mode to Occupied Mode) of any Zone Group served by the AHU

chunk.cleaned_text:
ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  143 5.16.14.10.  For each air handler, the operator shall be able to suppress the alarm for any fault condition.
5.16.14.11.  Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended under the following conditions:
a. When AHU is not operating
b. For a period of ModeDelay minutes f ollowing a change in mode (e.g., from Warmup  Mode to
Occupied Mode ) of any Zone Group  served by the AHU
5.16.14.12.  Fault conditions that are not applicable to the current OS shall not be evaluated.
5.16.14.13.  A fault condition that evaluates as TRUE  must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before it
is reported to the operator.
5.16.14.14.  Test mode shall temporarily set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for a period of
TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing of the AFDD system, and ensure normal fault
detection occurs afte r testing is complete.
5.16.14.15.

manual_entries.title: Freeze Protection Stage 1
manual_entries.summary: If SAT drops below 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two or more heating hot-water plant requests, override OA damper to minimum position, and modulate heating coil to maintain SAT of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable when SAT rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.

structured_payload.title: Freeze Protection Stage 1
structured_payload.summary: If SAT drops below 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two or more heating hot-water plant requests, override OA damper to minimum position, and modulate heating coil to maintain SAT of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable when SAT rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Send heating plant requests, set OA damper to minimum, modulate heating coil to maintain SAT ≥ 6°C; disable when SAT > 7°C for 5 minutes
structured_payload.evidence_quote: If the supply air temperature drops below 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, override the outdoor air damper to the minimum position, and modulate the heating coil to maintain a supply air temperature of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable this function when supply air temperature rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.

chunk.cleaned_text:
The third stage
shuts down the unit and activates coil valves and pumps to circulate water  in case the second stage does not work
(e.g., stuck economizer damper).
5.18.11.1.  If the supply air temperature drops below 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two (or more, as required
to ensure that heating plant is active) heat ing hot -water plant requests, override the outdoor air
damper to the minimum position, and modulate the heating coil to maintain a supply air
temperature of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable this function when supply air temperature rises above
7°C (45°F) for 5  minutes.
The first stage of freeze protection locks out the economizer. Most likely this has already occurred by this time, but
this logic provides insurance.
5.18.11.2.  If the supply air temperature drops below 3°C (38°F) for 5 minutes, fully close both the economi zer
damper and the minimum outdoor air damper for 1 hour and set a Level 3 alarm noting that
minimum ventilation was interrupted.
chunk.text_excerpt:
The third stage
shuts down the unit and activates coil valves and pumps to circulate water  in case the second stage does not work
(e.g., stuck economizer damper).
5.18.11.1.  If the supply air temper

evidence.evidence_text: If the supply air temperature drops below 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, override the outdoor air damper to the minimum position, and modulate the heating coil to maintain a supply air temperature of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable this function when supply air temperature rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.

ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  159 Stage 3 can be triggered by either of two conditions. The second condition is meant to respond to an extreme and
sudden cold snap.
Protecting the cooling coil in this situation will require water moveme nt through the coil, which means that the
CHW pumps need to be energized.
Heating coil is controlled to an air temperature setpoint . The sensors will not read accurately with the fan OFF, but
they will be influenced by proximity to the heating coil. A temperature of 27°C (80°F) at either of these sensors
indicates that the interior of the unit is sufficiently warm. This avoids the situation where a fixed valve position leads
to very high (and potentially damaging) temperatures inside the unit.

5.18.14.  Testing /Commissioning Overrides . Provide software switches that interlock to a CHW  and hot -water plant
level to
a. force HW valve full open if  there is a hot -water coil,
b. force HW valve full closed if  there is a hot -water coil,
c. force CHW valve full open if  there is a CHW  coil, and
d. force CHW  valve full closed  if there is a CHW coil.
Per Section 5.1.10 , all hardware points can be overridden through the BAS.

manual_entries.title: AFDD Operating State Definition
manual_entries.summary: The OS of each AHU shall be defined by the commanded positions of heating coil valve, cooling coil valve, and economizer damper per Table 5.18.13.2.

structured_payload.title: AFDD Operating State Definition
structured_payload.summary: The OS of each AHU shall be defined by the commanded positions of heating coil valve, cooling coil valve, and economizer damper per Table 5.18.13.2.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Determine OS based on valve and damper positions
structured_payload.evidence_quote: The OS of each AHU shall be defined by the commanded positions of the heating-coil control valve, cooling-coil control valve, and economizer damper in accordance with Table 5.18.13.2 and Figure 5.18.13.2.

chunk.cleaned_text:
The OS of each AHU shall be defined by the commanded positions of the heating -coil control
valve, cooling -coil control valve, and economizer damper in accordance with Table 5.18.13.2  and
Figure 5.18.13.2 .
Table 5.18.13.2 SZVAV AHU Operating States
Operating State  Heating  Valve Position  Cooling Valve Position  Outdoor Air Damper
Position
#1: Heating  > 0 = 0 = MinOA -P
#2: Free cooling, modulating OA  = 0 = 0 MinOA -P < x < 100%
#3: Mechanical + economizer cooling  = 0 > 0 = 100%
#4: Mechanical cooling, minimum OA  = 0 > 0 = MinOA -P
#5: Unknown or dehumidification  No other OS applies
Figure 5.18.13.2 SZVAV AHU operating states.
The OS is distinct from , and should not be confused with , the zone status (cooling, heating, deadband ) or Zone
Group  mode (occupied, warmup , etc.).
OS#1 through  OS#4  (see Table 5.18.13.2)  represent normal operation during which a fault may nevertheless occur
if so determined by the fault condition tests in Section 5.18.13.6 .

manual_entries.title: AFDD Evaluation Suspension Conditions
manual_entries.summary: Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended when AHU is not operating and for a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode of any Zone Group served by the AHU.

structured_payload.title: AFDD Evaluation Suspension Conditions
structured_payload.summary: Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended when AHU is not operating and for a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode of any Zone Group served by the AHU.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: AHU not operating or mode change
structured_payload.required_behavior: Suspend fault condition evaluation
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended under the following conditions: a. When AHU is not operating b. For a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode (e.g., from Warmup Mode to Occupied Mode) of any Zone Group served by the AHU

chunk.cleaned_text:
ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  143 5.16.14.10.  For each air handler, the operator shall be able to suppress the alarm for any fault condition.
5.16.14.11.  Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended under the following conditions:
a. When AHU is not operating
b. For a period of ModeDelay minutes f ollowing a change in mode (e.g., from Warmup  Mode to
Occupied Mode ) of any Zone Group  served by the AHU
5.16.14.12.  Fault conditions that are not applicable to the current OS shall not be evaluated.
5.16.14.13.  A fault condition that evaluates as TRUE  must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before it
is reported to the operator.
5.16.14.14.  Test mode shall temporarily set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for a period of

chunk.cleaned_text:
ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  151 outdoor air temperature. The goal of this scheme is to maximize free cooling and avoid chiller use when the outdoor
air is cool, while avoiding excessive fan en ergy use and using the cooling coil when outdoor air is warm.
For this to work, it is essential that both SATsp and SATsp -C are controlled off the same physical SAT sensor.
It is also critical that the minimum value of the setpoint  that controls the econom izer SATsp is lower than the
minimum value of the setpoint  that controls the CHW valve SATsp -C. Otherwise, a brief temperature excursion due
to the cooling coil will lead to short cycling of the economizer and subsequent unnecessary energy use by the
cooli ng coil.
5.18.4.1.  The supply fan shall run whenever the unit is in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode .
5.18.4.2.  Provide a ramp function to prevent changes in fan speed of more than 10% per minute.

manual_entries.summary: Medium fan speed MedSpeed is reset linearly based on outdoor air temperature from MinSpeed when OAT ≥ Endpoint #1 to MaxCoolSpeed when OAT ≤ Endpoint #2.

structured_payload.title: Medium Fan Speed Reset Based on OAT
structured_payload.section_title: Supply Fan Speed Control and Supply Air Temperature Set-Point Reset
structured_payload.summary: Medium fan speed MedSpeed is reset linearly based on outdoor air temperature from MinSpeed when OAT ≥ Endpoint #1 to MaxCoolSpeed when OAT ≤ Endpoint #2.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Reset MedSpeed linearly between MinSpeed and MaxCoolSpeed based on OAT, with endpoints defined by zone temperature and MaxDPT
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Medium fan speed MedSpeed shall be reset linearly based on outdoor air temperature from MinSpeed when outdoor air temperature is greater or equal to Endpoint #1 to MaxCoolSpeed when outdoor air temperature is less than or equal to Endpoint #2.

chunk.cleaned_text:
Minimum speed M inSpeed, maximum cooling speed MaxCoolSpeed, and maximum heating
speed MaxHeatSpeed shall be determined per Section 3.2.1 .
b. Medium fan speed MedSpeed  shall be reset linearly based on outdoor air temperature from
MinSpeed when outdoor air temperature is greater or equal to Endpoint #1 to MaxCoolSpeed
when outdoor air temperature is less than or equal to Endpoint #2.
1. Endpoint #1: the lesser of zone tempe rature +0.5°C (1°F) and maximum supply air dew
point MaxDPT.
2. Endpoint #2: the lesser of zone temperature minus 6°C (10°F) and the maximum supply air
dew point MaxDPT minus 1°C (2°F).
When outdoor air temperature is high, there is a potential for a high hum idity ratio, and thus high space humidity,
which can increase the risk of mold/mildew. Because dew point sensors are expensive and can quickly drift out of
calibration, this sequence uses outdoor air dry -bulb temperature as a proxy for supply air dew point .

a. The Deadband values of SATsp and SATsp -C shall be the average of the zone heating setpoint
and the zone cooling setpoint  but shall be no lower than 21°C (70°F) and no higher than 24°C
(75°F).
The deadband setpoint  is intended to pro vide neutral temperature air when the Zone State  is deadband. The values
of this setpoint  are limited to avoid the situation where an extreme value for zone temperature setpoint  forces
unnecessary heating or cooling, e.g., a cold -aisle setpoint  of 32°C (90 °F) in a datacenter could cause unnecessary
heating if this limit were not in place.

manual_entries.title: Fan Speed Reset in Heating and Cooling
manual_entries.summary: Fan speed is reset based on heating or cooling loop signal: from MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed for 100% to 50% heating, MinSpeed for 50% to 0% heating and deadband, MinSpeed for 0% to 25% cooling, reset from MinSpeed to MedSpeed for 25% to 50% cooling, MedSpeed for 50% to 75% cooling, and reset from MedSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed for 75% to 100% cooling.

structured_payload.title: Fan Speed Reset in Heating and Cooling
structured_payload.section_title: Supply Fan Speed Control and Supply Air Temperature Set-Point Reset
structured_payload.summary: Fan speed is reset based on heating or cooling loop signal: from MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed for 100% to 50% heating, MinSpeed for 50% to 0% heating and deadband, MinSpeed for 0% to 25% cooling, reset from MinSpeed to MedSpeed for 25% to 50% cooling, MedSpeed for 50% to 75% cooling, and reset from MedSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed for 75% to 100% cooling.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Heating or cooling loop signal value
structured_payload.required_behavior: Reset fan speed according to the specified ranges
structured_payload.evidence_quote: a. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed. b. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, fan speed setpoint is MinSpeed. c. In deadband, fan speed setpoint is MinSpeed. d. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 25%, fan speed is MinSpeed. e. For a cooling-loop signal of 25% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MinSpeed to MedSpeed. f. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 75%, fan speed is MedSpeed. g. For a cooling-loop signal of 75% to 100%, fan speed is reset from MedSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed.

by a separate control loop, but both loops use the same supply air temperature sensor.
5.18.5.2.  The control loop for SATsp is enabled when the supply air fan is proven ON and disabled and set to
neutral otherwise.
a. Supply air temperature shall be controlled to SATsp by a control loop whose output is mapped
to sequence the heating coil (if applicable) and economizer dampers as shown in the Figure
5.18.5.2. Outdoor air damp er minimum MinOA -P and maximum MaxOA -P positions are
limited for economizer lockout and to maintain minimum outdoor airflow rate as described in
Sections 5.18.6 and 5.18.6.3 .
These sequences assume that the heat source can be modulated and thus control SAT to a setpoint  in heating.

156 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  5.18.6.3.  Outdoor Air Damper Control for Units with an Outdoor Airflow Measurement Station
This section describes minimum outdoor air control logic for a unit with a single common minimum OA and
economizer damper (i.e., no separate minimum OA damper) and Demand Control Ventilation.
This logic assumes that there is an airflow measurement station across the outdoor air intake and controls OA
volume directly via control over the  minimum OA damper position.
Other configurations are possible and would require modifications to the points list (above) and the control logic
below.
a. Minimum outdoor air control loop is enabled when the supply fan is proven on and in
Occupied Mode  and dis abled and output set to zero otherwise.
b. The minimum outdoor airflow rate shall be maintained at the minimum outdoor air setpoint
MinOAsp by a reverse -acting control loop whose output is mapped to MinOA -P.
5.18.7.

chunk.cleaned_text:
Economizer Lockout
This section describes economizer lockout logic for a unit with a common minimum OA and economizer damper
(i.e., no separate minimum OA damper). Other configurations are possible, and would require modifications to the
points list (above) and the control l ogic below.
5.18.7.1.  The normal sequencing of the economizer dampers shall be disabled in accordance with Section
5.1.17 .
5.18.7.2.  Once the economizer is disabled, it shall not be r eenabled within 10 minutes and vice versa.
5.18.7.3.  When economizer is enabled, MaxOA -P = 100%. When economizer is disabled, set MaxOA -P
equal to MinOA -P. See Section 5.16.5 , “Supply Air Temperature Control,” and Section 5.18.6 ,
“Minimum Outdoor Air Control,” for outdoor air damper minimum setpoint .
The engineer must select among control logic options for return/relief/exhaust.

multiple -spaces procedure, compliance with the standard is assured. Doing no DCV at all is not an option , because

chunk.cleaned_text:
ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  151 outdoor air temperature. The goal of this scheme is to maximize free cooling and avoid chiller use when the outdoor
air is cool, while avoiding excessive fan en ergy use and using the cooling coil when outdoor air is warm.
For this to work, it is essential that both SATsp and SATsp -C are controlled off the same physical SAT sensor.
It is also critical that the minimum value of the setpoint  that controls the econom izer SATsp is lower than the
minimum value of the setpoint  that controls the CHW valve SATsp -C. Otherwise, a brief temperature excursion due
to the cooling coil will lead to short cycling of the economizer and subsequent unnecessary energy use by the
cooli ng coil.
5.18.4.1.  The supply fan shall run whenever the unit is in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode .

structured_payload.title: Supply Fan Speed and Supply Air Temperature Control in Heating Mode
structured_payload.section_title: Supply Fan Speed and Supply Air Temperature Control
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit with heating coil
structured_payload.summary: When Zone State is Heating, fan speed and SAT setpoint are controlled based on heating-loop signal ranges.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Zone State is Heating and there is a heating coil
structured_payload.required_behavior: For heating-loop signal 100% to 50%, FANsp is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinHeatSpeed; for 50% to 0%, FANsp is MinHeatSpeed. For heating-loop signal 100% to 50%, SATsp is Heat_SAT; for 50% to 0%, SATsp is reset from Heat_SAT to active zone heating setpoint. The heating coil is modulated with a PID loop to maintain discharge temperature at SATsp. Cooling coil is OFF.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: a. If there is a heating coil, when Zone State is Heating 1. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, FANsp is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinHeatSpeed. 2. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, FANsp is MinHeatSpeed. 3. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, SATsp is Heat_SAT. 4. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, SATsp is reset from Heat_SAT to the active zone heating setpoint. 5. The heating coil shall be modulated with a PID loop to maintain the discharge temperature at SATsp. 6. Cooling coil OFF

indicates that the interior of the unit is sufficiently warm. This avoids the situation where a fixed valve position leads

structured_payload.title: AFDD Required Points
structured_payload.summary: The following points must be available to AFDD routines: SAT, RAT (if present), SATsp, HC, CC, FS.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Provide SAT, RAT (if present), SATsp, HC (heating-coil valve position command 0-100%), CC (cooling-coil valve position command 0-100%), FS (fan-speed command 0-100%).
structured_payload.evidence_quote: The following points must be available to the AFDD routines for each FCU: a. SAT = supply air temperature b. RAT = return air temperature (if present) c. SATsp = supply air temperature setpoint d. HC = heating-coil valve position command; 0% ≤ HC ≤ 100% e. CC = cooling-coil valve position command; 0% ≤ CC ≤ 100% f. FS = fan-speed command; 0% ≤ FS ≤ 100%

ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  263 Table 5.20.6.2 FCU Operating States
Operating State  Heating Valve Position  Cooling Valve Position
#1: Heating  > 0 = 0
#2 No Heating or Cooling  = 0 = 0
#3: Cooling  = 0 > 0
#4 Unknown  No other OS applies
Figure 5.20.6.2 FCU operating states.
The OS is distinct from, and should not be confused with, the zone status (cooling, heating, deadband).
OS#1 through  OS#3  (see Table 5.20.6.2 ) represent normal operation during which a fault may nevertheless occur if
so determined by the fault condition tests in Section 5.18.13.6 . By contrast, OS#4 may represent  an abnormal or
incorrect condition (such as simultaneous heating and cool ing) arising from a controller failure or programming
error.

structured_payload.required_behavior: Calculate five-minute rolling averages with 1-minute sampling of SAT_avg, RAT_avg (if fitted), and ΔOS (number of changes in OS during previous 60 minutes). Operator shall have ability to adjust averaging window and sampling period for each point independently.

manual_entries.title: AFDD Internal Variables Default Values

structured_payload.title: AFDD Internal Variables Default Values
structured_payload.summary: Table 5.20.6.5 defines internal variables with default values: ΔTSF=0.5°C, εSAT=1°C, εRAT=1°C, ΔOSmax=7, ModeDelay=30 min, AlarmDelay=30 min, TestModeDelay=120 min.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Use default values as given; all parameters adjustable by operator.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Table 5.20.6.5 FCU Internal Variables Variable Name Description Default Value ΔTSF Temperature rise across supply fan 0.5°C (1°F) εSAT Temperature error threshold for SAT sensor 1°C (2°F) εRAT Temperature error threshold for RAT sensor 1°C (2°F) ΔOSmax Maximum number of changes in Operating State during the previous 60 minutes (moving window) 7 ModeDelay Time in minutes to suspend Fault Condition evaluation after a change in mode 30 AlarmDelay Time in minutes that a Fault Condition must persist before triggering an alarm 30 TestModeDelay Time in minutes that Test Mode is enabled 120

5.22.6.4.  The following values must be continuously calculated by the AFDD routines for each FCU:

shall have the ability to adjust the averaging window and sampling period for each point

1. SAT avg = rolling average of supply air temperature
2. RAT avg = rolling average of return air temperature (if fitted)
3. OS = number of chang es in OS during the previous 60 minutes (moving window)
5.22.6.5.  The internal variables shown in Table 5.20.6.5  shall be defined for each FCU. All parameters are
adjustable by the operator, with initial values as given below .
Default values are derived from NISTIR 7365 and have been validated in field trials.

structured_payload.summary: Operator shall be able to suppress the alarm for any fault condition.

5.22.6.8.  For each FCU, the operator shall be able to su ppress the alarm for any fault condition.
5.22.6.9.  Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended under the following conditions:
a. When FCU is not operating
b. For a period of ModeDelay  minutes following a change in mode (e.g., from Warmup  Mode or
Cooldown  Mode to Occupied  Mode ) of any Zone Group  served by the FCU.
5.22.6.10.  Fault conditions that are not applicable to the current OS shall not be evaluated.
5.22.6.11.  A fault condition that evaluates as true must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before it is
reported to the operator.
5.22.6.12.  Test mode shall temporarily set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for a period of
TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant test ing of the AFDD system and ensure normal fault
detection occurs after testing is complete.
5.22.6.13.

structured_payload.title: Chilled-Water Reset Requests Logic
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit with chilled-water coil
structured_payload.summary: Send reset requests based on SAT deviation and CHW valve position.
structured_payload.required_behavior: If SAT > setpoint by 10°F for 5 min, send 3 requests; else if SAT > setpoint by 5°F for 5 min, send 2 requests; else if CHW valve >95%, send 1 request until <85%; else send 0.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: b. If the supply air temperature is 10°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 3 requests, c. Else if the supply air temperature is 5°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 2 requests, d. Else if the CHW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the CHW valve position is less than 85%. e. Else if the CHW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

Dual-Duct VAV Terminal Unit  − Snap -Acting Control
Snap -acting control  logic is the first choice among the various DD control schemes , as it is the most efficient and
does not require DD boxes with mixing sections that have a high pressure drop. It allows use  of dual standard
airflow sensors, one at each inlet, with standard pressure independent logic blocks; alternatively, a single discharge
airflow sensor may be used.
However, snap -acting logic is not ideal for CO 2 control because it can cause the zone to os cillate between cooling
and heating.

manual_entries.title: Demand Limit Level Reset Condition
manual_entries.summary: Demand limit level switch shall be set when measured demand exceeds limit; reset when demand is less than 10% below limit for minimum 15 minutes and time is no longer within on-peak/partial-peak window.

structured_payload.title: Demand Limit Level Reset Condition
structured_payload.summary: Demand limit level switch shall be set when measured demand exceeds limit; reset when demand is less than 10% below limit for minimum 15 minutes and time is no longer within on-peak/partial-peak window.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Set demand-limit level switch; reset when demand < 90% of limit for 15 minutes and outside peak window.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: When the measured demand exceeds the limit, the demand-limit level switch for that level shall be set; when demand is less than 10% below the limit for a minimum of 15 minutes, and the time is no longer within the on-peak or partial-peak window, the switch shall be reset.

chunk.cleaned_text:
66 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  Demand limits can be triggered for different reasons, including initiating utility demand shed events, exceeding a
predefined threshold, or to prevent excessive rates in a ratchet schedule. Additional logic (not provided here) is
needed to define the demand -limit levels.
For example:
1. Sliding Window. The demand control function  shall use a sliding window method selectable in increments of 1
minute, up to 60 minutes, with a 15 -minute default.
2. Demand -Limit Levels. Demand time periods shall be set up as per utility rate schedule. For each on -peak or
partial -peak period, three d emand limits can be defined. When the measured demand exceeds the limit, the
demand -limit level switch for that level shall be set; when demand is less than 10% below the limit for a
minimum of 15 minutes, and the time is no longer within the on -peak or pa rtial-peak window, the switch shall
be reset.

structured_payload.title: Heating Loop Enable/Disable
structured_payload.summary: Heating Loop shall be enabled when space temperature is below heating setpoint and disabled when above heating setpoint and loop output is zero for 30 seconds.

chunk.cleaned_text:
68 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  a. The Heating Loop  shall be enabled whenever the space temperature is below the current zone
heating set-point  temperature and disabled when space temperature is above the current zone
heating setpoi nt temperature and the loop output is zero for 30 seconds. The loop may remain
active at all times if provisions are made to minimize integral windup.
b. The Cooling Loop  shall be enabled whenever the space temperature is above the current zone
cooling set-point temperature and disabled when space temperature is below the current zone
cooling set-point  temperature and the loop output is zero for 30 seconds. The loop may remain
active at all times if provisions are made to minimize integral windup.
5.3.4.2.  The Coolin g Loop  shall maintain the space temperature at the active cooling setpoint . The output of

manual_entries.title: Zone Group Definition
manual_entries.summary: Each system shall be broken into separate Zone Groups composed of a collection of one or more zones served by a single air handler.

structured_payload.title: Zone Group Definition
structured_payload.summary: Each system shall be broken into separate Zone Groups composed of a collection of one or more zones served by a single air handler.

chunk.cleaned_text:
ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  69
Zone Group s
Zone scheduling groups , or Zone Group s, are sets of zones served by a single air handler that operate together for
ease of scheduling and/or in order to ensure sufficient load to maintain stable operation in the upstream equipment.
A Zone Group  is equivalent to an isolation area  as defined in ASHRAE/IES Standard 90.1 2016, Section 6.4.3.3.4 .
5.4.1.  Each system shall be broken into separate Zone Group s composed of a collection of one or more zones
served by a single air handler. See Section 3.1.3  for Zone Group  assignments.
5.4.2.  Each Zone Group  shall be capable of having separate occupancy schedules and operating modes  from
other Zone Group s.
Note that, from the user’s point of view, schedules can be set for individu al zones, or they can be set for an entire
Zone Group , depending on how the user interface is implemented.

chunk.cleaned_text:
From the point of view of the BAS, individual
zone schedules are superimposed to create a zone-group  schedule, which then drives system behavior.
The schedule may govern operation of other integrated systems such as lights, daylighting, or other , in addition to
the HVAC system.
5.4.3.  All zones in each Zone Group  shall be in the same zone -group operating mode  as defined in Section 5.4.6 .
If one zone in a Zone Group  is placed in any zone -group operating mode  other than Unoccupied Mode
(due to override, sequence logic, or scheduled occupancy ), all zones in that Zone Gro up shall enter that
mode.
Occupied -standby mode applies to individual zones, is considered a zonal subset of Occupied Mode , and shall not
be considered a zone-group operating mode .
5.4.4.  A Zone Group  may be in only one mode at a given time.
5.4.5.  For each Zone Group , provide a set of testing/commissioning software switches that override all zones
served by the Zone Group .

manual_entries.title: DCV Not Provided for Cooling-Only Boxes Due to Overcooling Risk
manual_entries.summary: DCV logic is not provided for cooling-only boxes because it results in periods of overcooling due to CO2 levels rising faster than cooling load.

structured_payload.title: DCV Not Provided for Cooling-Only Boxes Due to Overcooling Risk
structured_payload.summary: DCV logic is not provided for cooling-only boxes because it results in periods of overcooling due to CO2 levels rising faster than cooling load.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: DCV logic is not provided for cooling-only boxes, because doing so results in periods of overcooling, as the CO2 levels due to occupants rises much faster than the cooling load due to occupants because of thermal mass.

chunk.cleaned_text:
ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  71 CO 2 DCV for cooling -only zones can lead to overcooling due to the faster rise in CO 2 levels from people in the room
versus the increase in cooling loads from people. Including heat in all zones with CO 2 DCV is therefore
recommended.
5.5.3.  See Section 3.1.2.1  for zone minimum airflow setpoint  Vmin , zone maximum cooling airflow setpoint
Vcool -max, and zone maximum heating airflow setpoint Vheat -max.
If the minimum ventilation rate is more than 25% or so of the cooling maximum, or DCV is used, a reheat box is
recommended to avoid overcooling. DCV logic is not provided for cooling -only boxes, because doing so results in
periods of overcooling, as the CO 2 levels due to  occupants rises much faster than the cooling load due to occupants
because of thermal mass.
Cooling -only terminal units can provide heating only when the AHU supply air temperature is more than 3°C (5°F)
above the room temperature.

manual_entries.title: Zone State Cooling Airflow Setpoint and Heating Coil Control
manual_entries.summary: When zone state is cooling, the cooling-loop output maps to active airflow setpoint between cooling minimum and cooling maximum endpoints. Heating coil is disabled unless DAT is below minimum setpoint. If AHU SAT > room temperature, active airflow setpoint is no higher than minimum endpoint.

structured_payload.title: Zone State Cooling Airflow Setpoint and Heating Coil Control
structured_payload.summary: When zone state is cooling, the cooling-loop output maps to active airflow setpoint between cooling minimum and cooling maximum endpoints. Heating coil is disabled unless DAT is below minimum setpoint. If AHU SAT > room temperature, active airflow setpoint is no higher than minimum endpoint.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Map cooling-loop output to active airflow setpoint from cooling minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint. Disable heating coil unless DAT is below minimum setpoint. If supply air temperature from air handler is greater than room temperature, set active airflow setpoint no higher than minimum endpoint.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: When the Zone State is cooling, the cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active airflow setpoint from the cooling minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. Heating coil is disabled unless the DAT is below the minimum setpoint (see Section 5.6.5.4). a. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.

The constant value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of t he
transducer and A/D converter used to measure airflow.  The value used should be determined as the minimum
accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.

(5°F), generate a  Level 4 alarm.

5.5.7.  Testing/Commissioning Overrides . Provide software switches that interlock to a system -level point to

d. force  damper full closed/open , and

a. force  zone airflow setpoint  to zero ,
b. force  zone airflow setpoint  to Vcool -max,

e. force  heating to OFF/closed ,

manual_entries.title: Cooling SAT Reset Requests from Zone
manual_entries.summary: Zone sends cooling SAT reset requests based on temperature exceedance and cooling loop output: 3 requests if zone temp exceeds cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes; 2 requests if by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes; 1 request if cooling loop > 95% until < 85%; 0 requests otherwise.

structured_payload.title: Cooling SAT Reset Requests from Zone
structured_payload.summary: Zone sends cooling SAT reset requests based on temperature exceedance and cooling loop output: 3 requests if zone temp exceeds cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes; 2 requests if by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes; 1 request if cooling loop > 95% until < 85%; 0 requests otherwise.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Zone temperature exceeds cooling setpoint or cooling loop > 95%
structured_payload.required_behavior: Send 3 requests if zone temp exceeds cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes; else send 2 requests if by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes; else send 1 request if cooling loop > 95% until < 85%; else send 0 requests.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: a. If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 3 requests. b. Else if the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 2 requests. c. Else if the Cooling Loop is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the Cooling Loop is less than 85%. d. Else if the Cooling Loop is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

manual_entries.title: Static Pressure Reset Requests from Zone
manual_entries.summary: Zone sends static pressure reset requests based on airflow and damper position: 3 requests if measured airflow < 50% of setpoint and damper > 95% for 1 minute; 2 requests if airflow < 70% of setpoint and damper > 95% for 1 minute; 1 request if damper > 95% until < 85%; 0 requests otherwise.

structured_payload.title: Static Pressure Reset Requests from Zone
structured_payload.summary: Zone sends static pressure reset requests based on airflow and damper position: 3 requests if measured airflow < 50% of setpoint and damper > 95% for 1 minute; 2 requests if airflow < 70% of setpoint and damper > 95% for 1 minute; 1 request if damper > 95% until < 85%; 0 requests otherwise.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Measured airflow below setpoint and damper position > 95%
structured_payload.required_behavior: Send 3 requests if measured airflow < 50% of setpoint while setpoint > 0 and damper > 95% for 1 minute; else send 2 requests if airflow < 70% of setpoint and damper > 95% for 1 minute; else send 1 request if damper > 95% until < 85%; else send 0 requests.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: a. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint while setpoint is greater than zero and the damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 3 requests. b. Else if the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint while setpoint is greater than zero and the damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 2 requests. c. Else if the damper position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the damper position is less than 85%. d. Else if the damper position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

manual_entries.title: Cooling Mode Primary Airflow Setpoint Mapping
manual_entries.summary: In cooling mode, the cooling-loop output is mapped to the active primary airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.

structured_payload.title: Cooling Mode Primary Airflow Setpoint Mapping
structured_payload.section_title: When the Zone State Is Cooling
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload.summary: In cooling mode, the cooling-loop output is mapped to the active primary airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.

5.7.6.  Alarms
5.7.6.1.  Low Primary Airflow
a. If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater
than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.
b. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater
than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm.
c. If a zone has an Importance -Multiplier  of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1 ) for its static pressure
reset T&R  control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone..

manual_entries.title: Airflow Sensor Calibration Threshold Determination
manual_entries.summary: The constant value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the transducer and A/D converter used to measure airflow. The value used should be determined as the minimum accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.

manual_entries.title: Designer Must Ensure Ventilation Compliance
manual_entries.summary: The designer must ensure that the sum of indirect ventilation provided by the fan plus the ventilation provided by the primary air at minimum setpoint meet Standard 62.1 requirements.

structured_payload.title: Designer Must Ensure Ventilation Compliance
structured_payload.section_title: When the Zone State Is Cooling
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, variable-volume fan
structured_payload.summary: The designer must ensure that the sum of indirect ventilation provided by the fan plus the ventilation provided by the primary air at minimum setpoint meet Standard 62.1 requirements.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Designer must verify that total ventilation (fan indirect + primary air at minimum setpoint) meets Standard 62.1 requirements.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: The designer must ensure that the sum of the indirect ventilation provided by the fan plus the ventilation provided by the primary air at minimum setpoint meet Standard 62.1 requirements.

chunk.cleaned_text:
If ventilation is according to California Title 24, the fan shall run in Deadband and Cooling
when the primary air volume is less than Zone -Abs-OA-min for 1 minute, and shall shut off
when primary air volume is above Zone -Abs-OA-min by 10% for 3 minutes.
The designer must ensure that the sum of the indirect ventilation provided by the fan plus the ventilation provided by
the primary air at minimum setpoint  meet Standard 62.1 requirements.
5.7.6.  Alarms
5.7.6.1.  Low Primary Airflow
a. If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater
than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.
b. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater
than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm.
c. If a zone has an Importance -Multiplier  of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1 ) for its static pressure
reset T&R  control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone..

5.8.5.2.  When the Zone State  Is Deadband
a. The active primary airflow setpoint  shall be the minimum endpoint .
b. Heating coil is OFF.
c. If ventilation is according to ASHRAE Standard 62.1 -2016, parallel fan runs if the active
primary airflow setpoint  is below Voz. Fan airflow rate setpoint  is equal to Voz minus the

d. If ventilation is according to California  Title 24, in Occupied Mode  only, parallel fan runs if
the active primary airflow setpoint  is below Zone -Abs-OA-min.

[90°F ] at 21 °C [70°F ] space temperature setpoint ) to minimize stratification.

manual_entries.title: Cooling Mode Parallel Fan Control for ASHRAE 62.1
manual_entries.summary: In cooling mode and Occupied Mode, if ventilation is per ASHRAE 62.1, the parallel fan starts when primary airflow drops below Voz minus half of Pfan-z and shuts off when primary airflow rises above Voz. Fan airflow setpoint equals Voz minus current primary airflow setpoint.

structured_payload.title: Cooling Mode Parallel Fan Control for ASHRAE 62.1
structured_payload.section_title: When the Zone State Is Cooling
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, variable-volume fan
structured_payload.summary: In cooling mode and Occupied Mode, if ventilation is per ASHRAE 62.1, the parallel fan starts when primary airflow drops below Voz minus half of Pfan-z and shuts off when primary airflow rises above Voz. Fan airflow setpoint equals Voz minus current primary airflow setpoint.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Zone State is Cooling AND Occupied Mode AND ventilation per ASHRAE 62.1
structured_payload.required_behavior: Start parallel fan when primary airflow < Voz - 0.5*Pfan-z; shut off when primary airflow > Voz. Set fan airflow setpoint = Voz - current primary airflow setpoint.

Fan airflow rate setpoint is equal to Voz minus the current primary airflow setpoint.

f. Unoccupied Mode

structured_payload.title: Hot-Water Reset Request - 3 Requests
structured_payload.section_title: If There Is a Hot-Water Coil, Hot-Water Reset Requests
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, variable-volume fan with hot-water coil
structured_payload.summary: If DAT is 17°C (30°F) less than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 3 requests.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Send 3 requests.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: a. If the DAT is 17°C (30°F) less than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 3 requests.

chunk.cleaned_text:
ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  77 5.6.8.3.  If There Is  a Hot -Water Coil, Hot -Water Reset Requests
a. If the DAT  is 17°C (30°F) less than setpoint  for 5 minutes, send 3 requests.
b. Else if the DAT  is 8°C (15°F) less than setpoint  for 5 minutes, send 2 requests.
c. Else if HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request  until the HW valve position is
less than 85 %.
d. Else if the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
5.6.8.4.  If There Is a Hot -Water Coil and Heating Hot -Water  Plant, Heating Hot -Water Plant Requests.
Send the heating hot -water plant that serves the zone a heating hot -water plant request as follows:
a. If the HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the HW valve position is less
than 10%.
b. Else if  the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

Dual-Duct VAV Terminal Unit  − Snap -Acting Control
Snap -acting control  logic is the first choice among the various DD control schemes , as it is the most efficient and
does not require DD boxes with mixing sections that have a high pressure drop. It allows use  of dual standard
airflow sensors, one at each inlet, with standard pressure independent logic blocks; alternatively, a single discharge
airflow sensor may be used.
However, snap -acting logic is not ideal for CO 2 control because it can cause the zone to os cillate between cooling
and heating.

Dual -Fan Dual -Duct Heating VAV A ir-Handling Unit
5.17.1.  Supply Fan Control
5.17.1.1.  Supply Fan Start/Stop
a. Fan shall run when system is in the Warmup Mode  and Setback Mode , and during Occupied
Mode  while there are any heating -fan requests with a minimum runtime of 15 minutes.
Delete the following section if an air handler serves dual -duct boxes that do not have hot -duct inlet airflow
sensors, i.e., those that have only a box discharge airflow sensor . This section  may also be deleted if there is a
supply AFMS.
b. Totalize current airflow rate from VAV boxes to a software point.
VAV box airflow rates are summed to obtain overall supply air rate without the need for an AFMS at the air -handler
discharge.  This is used only for display and diagnostics and filter alarm.

structured_payload.summary: If HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until HW valve position is less than 10%.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Send 1 request until HW valve position < 10%.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: a. If the HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the HW valve position is less than 10%.

chunk.cleaned_text:
ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  77 5.6.8.3.  If There Is  a Hot -Water Coil, Hot -Water Reset Requests
a. If the DAT  is 17°C (30°F) less than setpoint  for 5 minutes, send 3 requests.
b. Else if the DAT  is 8°C (15°F) less than setpoint  for 5 minutes, send 2 requests.
c. Else if HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request  until the HW valve position is
less than 85 %.
d. Else if the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
5.6.8.4.  If There Is a Hot -Water Coil and Heating Hot -Water  Plant, Heating Hot -Water Plant Requests.
Send the heating hot -water plant that serves the zone a heating hot -water plant request as follows:
a. If the HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the HW valve position is less
than 10%.
b. Else if  the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

Dual-Duct VAV Terminal Unit  − Snap -Acting Control
Snap -acting control  logic is the first choice among the various DD control schemes , as it is the most efficient and
does not require DD boxes with mixing sections that have a high pressure drop. It allows use  of dual standard
airflow sensors, one at each inlet, with standard pressure independent logic blocks; alternatively, a single discharge
airflow sensor may be used.
However, snap -acting logic is not ideal for CO 2 control because it can cause the zone to os cillate between cooling
and heating.

manual_entries.title: Deadband Mode Primary Airflow Setpoint
manual_entries.summary: In deadband mode, the active primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint and the heating coil is OFF.

structured_payload.title: Deadband Mode Primary Airflow Setpoint
structured_payload.section_title: When the Zone State Is Deadband
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload.summary: In deadband mode, the active primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint and the heating coil is OFF.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Set active primary airflow setpoint to minimum endpoint. Heating coil OFF.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: a. The active primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint. b. Heating coil is OFF.

chunk.cleaned_text:
Fan airflow rate setpoint  is equal to
Zone -Abs-OA-min minus the current primary airflow setpoint .
The designer must ensure that the sum of the indirect ventilation provided by the fa n plus the ventilation provided by
the primary air at minimum setpoint  meet Standard 62.1 requirements.
5.8.5.2.  When the Zone State  Is Deadband
a. The active primary airflow setpoint  shall be the minimum endpoint .
b. Heating coil is OFF.
c. If ventilation is according to ASHRAE Standard 62.1 -2016, parallel fan runs if the active
primary airflow setpoint  is below Voz. Fan airflow rate setpoint  is equal to Voz minus the
active primary airflow setpoint .
d. If ventilation is according to California  Title 24, in Occupied Mode  only, parallel fan runs if
the active primary airflow setpoint  is below Zone -Abs-OA-min.

ASHRAE/IES Standard 90.1 -2016 limits overhead supply air to 11°C (20°F) above space temperature (e.g., 32°C
[90°F ] at 21°C [70°F ] space temperature setpoint) to minimize stratification.
a. The active primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.

maximum of MaxT above space temperature setpoint .
c. The heating coil shall be modulated to maintain the discharge temperature at setpoint . (Directly
controlling heating off zone temperature control loop is not acceptable).

structured_payload.title: VAV Damper Modulation to Maintain Airflow Setpoint
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload.summary: The VAV damper shall be modulated to maintain the measured airflow at setpoint.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Modulate VAV damper to maintain measured airflow at active primary airflow setpoint.

Zone Group  is in Occupied Mode . Prior to starting the fan, the damper is first driven fully closed to
ensure that the fan is n ot rotating backward.  Once the fan is proven ON for a fixed time delay (15
seconds), the damper override is released.

than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm.
c. If a zone has an Importance -Multiplier  of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1 ) for its static pressure
reset T&R  control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.

manual_entries.title: Fan Control Logic
manual_entries.summary: Fan shall run whenever zone is in heating or cooling Zone State, or if the associated Zone Group is in Occupied Mode. Prior to starting the fan, the damper is first driven fully closed to ensure the fan is not rotating backward. Once the fan is proven ON for a fixed time delay (15 seconds), the damper override is released.

manual_entries.title: Cooling SAT Reset Requests
manual_entries.summary: Send requests for cooling SAT reset based on zone temperature exceeding cooling setpoint or cooling loop output. If zone temp exceeds cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes, send 3 requests. If by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes, send 2 requests. If cooling loop > 95%, send 1 request until < 85%. Else send 0 requests.

structured_payload.title: Cooling SAT Reset Requests
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload.summary: Send requests for cooling SAT reset based on zone temperature exceeding cooling setpoint or cooling loop output. If zone temp exceeds cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes, send 3 requests. If by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes, send 2 requests. If cooling loop > 95%, send 1 request until < 85%. Else send 0 requests.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Zone temperature or cooling loop conditions
structured_payload.required_behavior: Send 3, 2, 1, or 0 requests as per conditions.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: a. If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 3 requests. b. Else if the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 2 requests. c. Else if the Cooling Loop is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the Cooling Loop is less than 85%. d. Else if the Cooling Loop is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
